promise_text: We will firmly continue our policy of ‘Zero Tolerance’ against terrorism and extremism.
We will continue to follow our policy of giving a free hand to our security forces in combating terrorism.
We will speed up the purchases of outstanding defense related equipments and weapons.
We will continue to take focused steps to strengthen the strike capability of the Armed Forces.
We are committed to focus on 'Make in India in Defence' to enable indigenous production of defence equipment.
We promise to create a more effective framework for the resettlement of our Armed Forces veterans.
The Armed Forces will start planning for the resettlement of soldiers three years before their retirement and in accordance with their preferences.
We will provide provision for skills training, soft skills training, financial support for higher education, housing, and starting an enterprise for Armed Forces veterans.
We will provide assistance to the states to upgrade their police forces through the ‘Scheme for Modernization of Police Forces’.
We will encourage expedited police reforms in the states so as to enable the State police forces to deal with new types of crimes like cyber crime and help them to be more sensitive to the citizens.
We will continue to take forward the process of modernizing the Central Armed Police Forces to further increase their capacity and readiness.
We will expedite the National Register of Citizens process in these areas on priority.
In future we will implement the NRC in a phased manner in other parts of the country.
We will continue to undertake effective steps to prevent illegal immigration in the North Eastern states.
We will further strengthen our border security.
We will implement the pilot project on the use of technology to strengthen border security (Smart Fencing) on all borders.
We will focus on creation of developmental and other necessary infrastructure in border areas.
We will further ease the movement of goods and people by constructing 14 more integrated check-posts by 2024.
We will continue to take steps for ensuring security of India’s long coastline.
We are committed to the enactment of the Citizenship Amendment Bill for the protection of individuals of religious minority communities from neighbouring countries escaping persecution.
Hindus, Jains, Buddhists, Sikhs and Christians escaping persecution from India’s neighbouring countries will be given citizenship in India.
We are committed to taking necessary and effective steps against left wing extremism to eliminate this menace in the next five years.
We will continue to make efforts in the direction of development of economic and social infrastructure in the tribal areas affected by left wing extremism.
We are committed to overcome all obstacles that come in the way of development and provide adequate financial resources to all the regions of the state of Jammu & Kashmir.
We will abrogate Article 370.
We are committed to annulling Article 35A of the Constitution of India.
We will take all steps to ensure a safe and peaceful environment for all residents of the state of Jammu & Kashmir.
We will make all efforts to ensure the safe return of Kashmiri Pandits.
We will provide financial assistance for the resettlement of refugees from West Pakistan, Pakistan occupied Jammu and Kashmir (POJK) and Chhamb.
We will make all efforts to achieve the goal of doubling farmers’ income by 2022.
We will provide short-term new agriculture loans up to Rs. 1 lakh at a 0% interest rate for 1-5 years on the condition of prompt repayment of the principal amount.
We will launch a pension scheme for all small and marginal farmers in the country so as to ensure social security to them on reaching 60 years of age.
We will make enrolment of farmers under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana voluntary.
We will further expand the coverage of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana to all the farmers in the country.
We will ensure timely availability of improved seeds of promising varieties at affordable rates with doorstep testing facilities.
We are committed to making an investment of Rs. 25 lakh crore to improve the productivity of the farm sector.
We will work towards reducing all agricultural imports and institute a predictable export and import policy with a built-in mechanism for encouraging exports and discouraging imports.
We will build an efficient storage and transport mechanism for agriculture produce.
We will roll out a new Village Storage Scheme of agri-produce.
We will provide farmers with loans at cheaper rates on the basis of storage receipt of the agri-produce.
We will launch a new mission to achieve self-sufficiency in oil seeds and other agri-products.
We will establish a National Warehousing Grid along National Highways to ensure necessary logistical linkages for warehousing of agricultural produce.
We will promote chemical-free organic farming in an additional 20 lakh hectare of hilly, tribal and rainfed areas in the next 5 years.
We will launch a dedicated e-commerce portal to enhance the availability of organic produce to the doorsteps of consumers.
Goshalas in the country will be linked to the promotion of organic farming.
We will promote organic eco-tourism in the vicinity of organic farming to ensure additional income to the farmers.
We will launch a National Bee-keeping and Honey Mission to ensure additional returns to the farmers.
We will double the honey production from the present 1,15,000 MT by developing infrastructural facilities and providing marketing support.
We will bring one crore hectares agricultural land under micro-irrigation.
We will supplement micro-irrigation with ‘fertigation’ to promote judicious use of fertilizers.
We will further expand Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchai Yojana to realize 100% irrigation potential of the country within a defined time-frame.
We will enable the creation of 10,000 new Farmer Producer Organisations (FPOs) by 2022 to ensure economies of scale for farmers regarding access to inputs and markets.
To cater to the needs of big cities we will set up a mechanism of direct marketing of vegetables, fruits, dairy and fishery products through farmers’ cooperative organizations.
We will create a mobile app based system for promoting the availability of agri-implements on rental/custom hiring basis.
We will encourage solar farming on a massive scale to provide an additional source of income for farmers.
We will complete digitization of land records on a mission mode.
We will implement second-generation land reforms to ensure title guarantee for the landholder and reduce land-related litigation.
We will create a model law for conclusive titling (in consultation with states) promoting title guarantee and title insurance and work with States to implement this system.

We will enable development of young agri-scientists to take advantage of Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Blockchain technology, Big Data analytics etc. for more predictive and profitable precision agriculture.
We will extensively use technology to ensure a better knowledge of the market prices of various agro-products for the benefit of farmers.
We will establish a network of mobile veterinary dispensaries to provide doorstep service to farmers.
We will roll out a model programme for periodic health checkup of all cattle and other domestic animals at the district level.
We will expand the coverage of immunization and eliminate Foot and Mouth Disease and Brucellosis.
We will launch a National Feed and Fodder Mission to eliminate the shortage of fodder.
We will promote aquaculture through easy access to credit.
We will launch ‘Matsya Sampada Yojana’ with an allocation of Rs. 10,000 crores to ensure availability of storage and marketing tools and infrastructure for small and traditional fishermen.
We will facilitate farming of sea-weed, pearl as well as ornamental fish to ensure a better return to the fishermen.
We will bring all fishermen under the ambit of all welfare programmes and social security schemes with expanded coverage for accident insurance.
We will launch ‘Jal Jivan Mission’ under which we will introduce a special program, ‘Nal se Jal’ to ensure piped water connection to every household by 2024.
We will launch a massive ‘Rural Road Upgradation Programme’ to connect centres of education, healthcare centres, and markets with hinterlands to promote rural growth.
We will ensure that every Gram Panchayat is connected through a high speed optical fibre network by 2022.
We will ensure a pucca house to every family who are living in a kuchha house by 2022.
We will ensure 100% disposal of liquid waste water and reuse of waste water.
We commit to make India a US$ 5 trillion economy by 2025 and US$ 10 trillion economy by 2032.
We will continue with our policy of lowering of tax rate thereby rewarding honest tax payers and improving compliance.
We will continue with the simplification of GST process by engaging in dialogue with all stakeholders.
By 2024, we will make capital investment of Rs.100 lakh crore in the infrastructure sector.
We will take the country’s ranking into the top 50 in the Ease of Doing Business Index.
We will announce a new industrial policy to improve competitiveness of manufacturing and services with an eye on Industry 4.0.
We will invest in creating clusters/networks that can take on the world’s best.
We will amend the Companies Act to impose civil liability for technical and procedural defaults of a minor nature.
We would aim to take the credit guarantee figure to Rs.1,00,000 crore by 2024.
We would achieve more than 150 Technology Centres all over the country by 2024.
On the lines of Kisan credit card, we will also create a scheme to give merchant credit card to registered merchants.
We will establish National Traders’ Welfare Board and create a National Policy for Retail Trade for the growth of retail businesses.
To protect the interests of small traders, we will provide an accident insurance of 10 lakh rupees to all the traders registered under GST.
We will launch a new scheme to provide collateral-free credit up to ₹50 lakh for entrepreneurs.
We will guarantee 50% of the loan amount for female entrepreneurs and 25% of the loan amount for male entrepreneurs.
We will initiate ranking of Central Ministries, departments, State Governments and CPSUs for their increased engagement with start-ups.
We will target time spent for tax compliance at 1 hour per month.
We will ease regulatory requirement for start-ups.
We will create 100 Innovation Zones in Urban Local Bodies.
We will set up 500 new incubators and accelerators by 2024.
We will facilitate establishment of 50,000 new Start ups in the nation by 2024.
We will continue to promote and encourage Start ups through creation of a ‘Seed Start up Fund’ of ₹20,000 crore.
We will set-up new 'Entrepreneurial North East' scheme to provide financial support to the micro, small and medium industries and for employment generation in the North Eastern state.

We will support entrepreneurial ventures started by individuals from Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Communities, or Economically Weaker Sections and take forward the ‘Stand-up India’ initiative.
We will identify specific places of cultural or natural importance in order to develop them as comprehensive destinations.
All UNESCO heritage sites in India will be upgraded to international level facilities.
We will continue our crackdown on Benami properties and illegal foreign bank accounts.
We will also expedite our actions to bring fugitive economic offenders back to India and prosecute them for their crimes.
We will ensure faster customs clearance of international cargo by relaxing clearance procedures, introducing self-declaration etc and adopting new scanning technology.
We will also provide adequate financial and institutional support for exporters and export organizations to build capacity.
We will aim at next generation infrastructure which will include gas grids and water grids, i-ways, regional airports and way side amenities along National highways.
We will launch a National Urban Mobility Mission to provide technology based urban mobility solutions to all urban local bodies.
Under this mission, we will incentivize cities to integrate the public transport systems.
We will also promote a common mobility card/ ticketing across different modes of transport.
In the next five years, we will ensure that 50 cities are covered with a strong metro network.
We will set up five regional centres of excellence on urban issues.
We will take the Swachh Bharat Mission to a new level through sustainable Solid Waste Management in every village.
Through the mission, we will ensure 100% disposal of liquid waste through emphasis on faecal sludge management and reuse of waste water in rural, peri urban and unsewered areas.
We will ensure that all habitations attain open defecation free status and those that have attained the status sustain the behavioural change.
We will ensure sustainability of water supply through special focus on conservation of rural water bodies and ground water recharge.
We will form a new Ministry of Water unifying the water management functions.
The Ministry will expeditedly take forward the ambitious programme for linking rivers from different parts of the country.
We will initiate work on the river linking programme by constituting an authority.
In the next five years, we will construct 60,000 kms of National Highways.
We will double the length of National Highways by 2022.
We will complete the Phase-1 of Bharatmala Project expeditiously.
We will also launch Bharatmala 2.0 project especially with a view to support the states to develop state road networks.
We will bring in new technologies and designs in road construction, operation and maintenance.
We will launch a programme for promoting clean energy and battery operated vehicles to become a world leader in e-mobility.
We will complete the dedicated freight corridor project by 2022.
We will launch a massive programme for modernization of railway stations in the country.
We will encourage private participation in the development of railway amenities and infrastructure and provision of services.
We will make all efforts to ensure electrification of all railway tracks by 2022.
We will equip all main railway stations with Wi-Fi facility by 2022.
We will ensure conversion of all viable rail tracks to broad gauge by 2022.
We will expand connectivity and experience of high speed trains and new version trains such as Vande Bharat Express across the length and breadth of the country in the next five years.
In the next five years, we will double the number of functional airports.
We will double our port capacity in the next five years.
We will further encourage integrated development of coastal areas including coastal cities, coastal transport and coastal industrialization.
We will undertake the upgradation of infrastructure for connecting the coast and hinterland to ensure smooth transportation.
We will continue to focus on development of inland waterways for shifting inland cargo movement from road and rail to water transport.
We will ensure speedy completion of the projects under the Sagarmala programme.
We will further work towards completing any unfinished task in providing 24x7 electricity.
We will ensure a right mix of energy which leads towards a cleaner environment.
We will ensure supplying quality electricity to all consumers.
We will ensure making the state electricity entities financially sound and administratively more efficient.
We will continue our efforts to achieve our goal of 175 GW renewable energy capacity by 2022.
We will further make all efforts to invite other countries of the world to become members of the International Solar Alliance.
Through village level high speed broadband services, we will provide a range of services such as telemedicine, tele-education and agriculture related advisories.
We will target provisioning of telemedicine and diagnostic laboratory facilities at all 1,50,000 Health and Wellness Centres by 2022.
We will create an essential devices list and a separate pricing policy for medical devices to ensure their accessibility and affordability to masses.
We will also accelerate the reforms in paramedical education sector so as to increase the availability of nurses, pharmacists and other paramedical personnel.
We will set up one Medical College or Post Graduate Medical College in every district, through public or private participation, by 2024.
To start with 75 such Medical Institutes will be set up by 2022.
We will double the number of MBBS and Specialist doctors in the country by 2024 as compared to the work done from 1947 till 2014.
We will endeavour to make the National Nutrition Mission a mass movement and strengthen infrastructure and capacity in all Anganwadis.
We will ensure full immunization coverage for all the children and pregnant women by 2022.
We will eliminate TB from India by 2025 through a Special Mission.
We are committed to the idea of simultaneous elections for Parliament, State assemblies and local bodies.
We will strive to ensure a common voter list for all elections.
We will continue to make efforts to ensure more effective governance and transparent decision making.
We will bring reform in the Civil Services and implement it in a manner to achieve 'Minimum Government and Maximum Governance'.
We will merge similar and complementary departments into sectoral ministries.
We will continue to implement the Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan in a focussed manner to build the capacity of members of Panchayati Raj Institutions.
We will formulate a ‘Model Police Act’ in consultation with the States to have a pro-people citizen friendly police.
We will work towards simplifying procedural laws, encouraging mediation and strengthening judicial and court management system in order to increase accessibility.
We will increase the number of Alternative Dispute Resolution forums to ensure avenues for quick disposal of matters.
We will also take all necessary steps to make India a hub of arbitration services.
We will expedite the enactment of legislation for creating an International Financial Services Centre Authority.
We will continue and further increase the ongoing incentives to expand digital transactions.
We will continue to pursue cooperative federalism by ensuring greater involvement of the states in all aspects of policy making and governance.
We will also ensure implementation of the 14th Finance Commission’s recommendations.
We will set up a committee for Easing Citizens’ Interactions with Government (CECIG) to work with experts in reforming citizen’s interface with Government.
We will explore every avenue to implement the Right to Service.

We will launch a new Science Mission for development of cutting edge technologies and future technologies with two submissions, specifically focused on (a) artificial intelligence mission and (b) robotic research mission.
We will launch a Language Translation Mission that will enable ready translation of English text in Science & Technology and humanities in any of our languages.
We will initiate a genome mission for human health that will bring the latest diagnosis and treatment to our entire population through frugal innovation.
We will explore the diversity of our oceans, have a human crewed submersible going to the deepest oceans.
We will get the latest technologies to transform Waste to Energy and Wealth in a major mission.
We will also strive towards semiconductor manufacturing within the country.
We will aim to spread S&T investment to the 95% of students who attend state government institutions also through a National Research Foundation in partnership with States.
Industry investment in R&D will also be enhanced, in partnership with our network of national laboratories.
We are committed to ensuring that 'Gaganyaan' successfully demonstrates our scientific capabilities of executing a manned mission in space.
We will start the 'National Program for Rapid Research in Socially Contextual Technologies' to create research paths in desirable technologies.
We are committed to maintaining the pace of adding to the forest cover through adoption of cleaner practises.
We are committed to continuing our work in providing basic amenities to people living in remotest forest areas.
We will convert the National Clean Air Plan into a Mission and we will focus on 102 most polluted cities in the country.
Through concerted action, we will reduce the level of pollution in each of the mission cities by at least 35% over the next five years.
We will ensure the Himalayan States are provided special financial assistance in the form of a 'Green Bonus' to facilitate the protection and promotion of forests in those states.
We will preserve the unique identity of Andaman-Nicobar and Lakshadweep Islands and build a world-class infrastructure compatible with the local ecosystem.
We will focus on fully developing the potential of tourism in these islands.
We will develop Union Territories as models of development and good governance.
We will leverage the situation to ensure accelerated development of the North Eastern states.
We are committed to ensure that the unique linguistic, cultural and social identity of the North Eastern States is adequately protected.
We will continue to focus on the development of infrastructure and improved connectivity in this region.
We will continue to take the necessary steps to leverage the tremendous potential of hydro-electric, tourism, horticulture etc in the North Eastern states.
We will create new opportunities of employment by providing more support to the 22 major 'Champion Sectors'.
We will optimally leverage the untapped employment-generation of potential of sectors such as defence and pharmaceuticals.
We will launch a special awareness and treatment programme for de-addiction among youth.
We will support a large scale internship programme for the youth in urban local bodies.
We will incentivise and reward self-organized groups of youth who adopt social assets like schools, hospitals, lakes, public gardens etc.
We will continue to provide adequate resources under the 'Khelo India' scheme.
Under this scheme, special attention will be given to encourage sporting talent among women and tribals.
We will map state and district-wise sport talent and strengths, recognize the traditional sporting practices and focus on the promotion of specific sports in specific regions.
We will introduce practical sports courses as part of the educational curriculum.
We will establish a National Sports Education Board for the development of sportspersons.
We will support the creation of a mini-stadium in each sub-district, with the participation of state governments.
We will formulate a mechanism to ensure that the capacity of existing sporting infrastructure is easily accessible for use of sportspersons and new models including PPP will be explored.
We will promote the Fit India campaign as an integral part of building a culture of sports in India.
We are committed to continue to build on the gains of 'Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao' to provide accessible and affordable quality education to all women.
We will ensure that ample financial support is available to girls through out their education and subsidised education loans are provided for higher education.
To generate better work opportunities for women, 10% material to be sourced for government procurement will be done from MSMEs having at least 50% women employees in their workforce.
We will now strengthen the creche programme with special focus on the needs of parents employed in the unorganized sector.
We will ensure that the number of childcare facilities is increased three fold by 2022.
We will formulate a comprehensive ‘Women in Workforce’ roadmap focussed on dramatically increasing the female workforce participation rate over the next five years.
We will also encourage industries and corporates to generate better employment opportunities for women.
We will make every effort to ensure access to credit and other resources, capacity building, market and distribution network for women entrepreneurs, SHGs and women farmers.
We will legislate a bill to prohibit and eliminate practices such as Triple Talaq and Nikah Halala.
We will focus on ensuring good quality, easily accessible and affordable maternal healthcare services for all women.
We will reduce the malnutrition level by at least 10% in the next five years under the POSHAN Abhiyan.
We will ensure that all reproductive and menstrual health services are easily available to all women across India.
With the expansion of ongoing Suvidha scheme, sanitary pads at an affordable cost of just Rs. 1 will be provided to all women and girls.
We will increase the coverage of Ayushman Bharat to include all Anganwadi workers and ASHA workers.
Gender sensitisation courses will be made an essential part of all educational institution’s curriculum and training modules of public offices.
We will formulate a dedicated programme for the creation of work opportunities, provide skill training and strengthen social security mechanism for widows of our Martyrs.
We will expand forensic facilities and fast track courts to bring convicts to justice in cases of rape.
The BJP is committed to 33% reservation in parliament and state assemblies through a constitutional amendment.
We will implement the 10% EWS reservation law in the same spirit while ensuring that SCs, STs and OBCs continue to have their fair representation.
We are committed to ensure benefits of constitutional provisions of the Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes and backward classes.
Every block with more than 50% ST population and at least 20,000 tribal persons will have an Eklavya Model Residential School.
We will ensure that the rights of all forest dwellers are fully protected.
We will establish 50,000 ‘Van Dhan Vikas Kendras’ in the tribal areas of the country.
We will make adequate provisions for personal safety equipment and accelerate the adoption of mechanized cleaning to ensure occupational health and safety for Safai Karamcharis.
We are committed to bringing down the percentage of families living below the poverty line to a single digit in the next five years.
We will take further steps to ensure access to bank branches, payment banks and banking correspondents by creating a new data-sharing framework.
With this scheme, we will ensure that every Indian has access to banking facilities within a radius of 5 km.
We will also launch a technology-enabled programme for broader financial literacy.
We will further widen the food security cover to provide subsidized sugar (at Rs. 13 per kg per family per month) to these families.
We are committed to further revise the tax slabs and the tax benefits to ensure more cash and greater purchasing power in the hands of our middle-income families.
We will make all efforts to ensure that our aspirational middle class has access to education, employment opportunities and suitable urban infrastructure.
We intend to continue with our effort to ensure that 115 aspirational districts catch up with the rest of the country.
We intend to continue our efforts to resolve the geographical imbalance of Eastern India and ensure that Eastern India is an equal participant in the progress and growth of the nation.
We are committed to the empowerment and ‘development with dignity’ of all minorities.
We will strengthen the Rashtriya Vayoshri Yojana to ensure that poor senior citizens in need of aids and assistive devices receive them in a timely manner.
Anganwadi and pre-school system will be strengthened to ensure that cases of disabilities are detected at an early stage.
We will put in place a system of continuous accessibility audits and ratings for cities, public infrastructure including airports, railway stations and public transport systems.
We will prioritize Divyang beneficiaries under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana and mandate accessibility benchmarks to be met.
We will offer higher interest rates on fixed deposits by divyangs.
We will recognise the 11 left out Indian Gorkha sub-tribes as Schedule Tribes.
We are committed to implement the reservation in the legislative assembly of Sikkim for Limboo and Tamang tribes.
We are committed to work towards finding a permanent political solution to the issue of Darjeeling Hills, Siliguri Terai and Dooars region.
We will maintain the same direction over the next five years to ensure a respectable living for the workers under the National Minimum Wage.
We will expand the Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maandhan scheme to cover all small shopkeepers.

We will create an umbrella scheme ‘Pradhan Mantri Kalanidhi Yojana’ to allow flexible and customized packages of credit, working capital and social security options to provide comprehensive support to traditional arts.
We will formulate a comprehensive Child Protection Framework to facilitate the setting up of standards and robust inspection and monitoring of child care institutions across India.
We are committed to bring transgenders to the mainstream through adequate socio-economic and policy initiatives.
We will ensure self-employment and skill development avenues for transgender youth.
We will explore all possibilities within the framework of the Constitution and all necessary efforts to facilitate the expeditious construction of the Ram Temple in Ayodhya.
We are committed to conserve and promote all culturally, religiously and spiritually significant heritage sites related to every faith in India and the ongoing PRASAD scheme will be expanded.
We will constitute a National Task Force to study the status of all written and spoken languages and dialects in India.
We will also work towards revival and promotion of vulnerable or extinct dialects and languages.
We will ensure that the teaching of Sanskrit is expanded and popularised at the school level.
We will also provide 100 Panini fellowships to researchers and scholars across India to promote research in Sanskrit.
The villages located on the banks of the river, which are already open defecation free, will be taken up under a special project to ensure complete sustainable management of solid and liquid waste.
We are committed to ensure a clean and uninterrupted flow of river Ganga from Gangotri to Ganga Sagar.
We will ensure that the sewerage infrastructure to deal with 100% of the waste water from the Ganga towns is completed and is functioning effectively.
We will undertake every effort to ensure that the subject of faith, tradition and worship rituals related to Sabarimala are presented in a comprehensive manner before the Hon’ble Supreme Court.
We will endeavour to secure constitutional protection on issues related to faith and belief.
We will further continue our efforts to promote Yoga globally.
We will undertake a rapid expansion of Yoga health hubs, Yoga tourism and research in Yoga.
An International Cultural Festival will be organised on a grand scale every year in 5 different states to showcase and promote the rich diversity of Indian culture.
We will launch a web-enabled virtual tour of all culturally and historically significant heritage sites and museums in India through ‘Dharohar Dharshan-an integrated web portal’.
The BJP reiterates its stand to draft a Uniform Civil Code, drawing upon the best traditions and harmonizing them with the modern times.
We will create an institutional mechanism to deepen the relationship of culture and heritage with people of Indian origin.
We will simultaneously launch ‘Bharat Gaurav’ campaign to increase interaction among the Indian diaspora.
We will strengthen the MADAD portal as single-point avenue for information and services for Indians living abroad.
We will effectively pursue co-operation against global evils, such as terrorism and corruption through forums like UN, the G20, BRICS, SCO, Commonwealth, etc.
Significant interactions such as Russia-India-China (RIC) and Japan-America-India (JAI) will be strengthened.
To forward our ‘Neighbourhood First’ policy, we will extensively leverage forums such as BIMSTEC.
Act East Policy, cooperation with ASEAN and ensuring an open, inclusive, prosperous and secure Indo-Pacific will be pursued vigorously.
We will strengthen our role as ‘first responder’ for disaster relief and humanitarian assistance and develop partnerships for disaster resilient infrastructure.
We will make knowledge exchange and transfer of technology for the development of all countries a major focus of our diplomatic relations.
We will endeavour to create an ‘International Space Technology Alliance’ for better coordination and cooperation on issues related to space technology.
We are committed to taking concrete steps on international forums against countries and organizations supporting terrorism.
We will work towards establishing a ‘Comity of Nations Against International Terrorism’ as a voluntary multi-lateral forum.
We are committed to seeking permanent membership of the United Nation Security Council.
We will increase the strength of the diplomatic and allied cadres.
We will facilitate the participation of experts in foreign policy-making through a robust mechanism.
We will establish a full-fledged University of Foreign Policy.
India’s states will be actively assisted in their international outreach for economic development.
Ensure adequate market avenues for the realisation of MSP through e-NAM, GrAMs and Pradhan Mantri AASHA Yojana.
Increase the cropped area under irrigation.
Enable the creation of 10,000 new Farmer Producer Organizations.
Work towards ensuring that maximum farmers get income support under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana.
Work towards completing all irrigation projects under the Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana.
Work towards increasing the number of seats in premier law institutes.
Work towards increasing the number of seats in premier engineering institutes.
Work towards assisting the maximum number of fishermen by ensuring the availability of storage and other infrastructure under a new ‘Matsya Sampada Yojana’.
Enable investment of ₹ 1 lakh crore in higher education through Revitalising of Infrastructure and Systems in Education (RISE).
Aim to cover all the secondary schools under Operation Digital Board.
Work towards digitising land records.
Ensure the LPG gas cylinder connection to all poor rural households.
Strive to ensure all-weather connectivity for every village.
Work towards increasing the number of seats in premier management institutes.
Ensure a pucca house to every family.
Ensure 100% electrification of all households.
Establish at least one Atal Tinkering Lab in every block.
Ensure every citizen has access to a bank account.
Ensure a toilet in every household.
Ensure access to safe and potable drinking water for all households.
Ensure the supply of piped cooking gas in major Tier 1 and 2 cities.
Increase port capacity to 2,500 MTPA.
Form a new Ministry of Water unifying the water management functions to approach the issue holistically and ensure better co-ordination of efforts.
Strive to achieve 10% blending of ethanol in petrol.
Achieve 175 GW of renewable energy capacity.
Aim to increase the number of operational airports to 150 for better air connectivity.
Ensure ODF status for all villages and cities.
Start developing small railway stations across India.
Establish 1.5 lakh health and wellness centres under Ayushman Bharat.
Start setting up 75 new medical colleges or post graduate medical colleges.
Ensure 100% waste collection under Swachh Bharat Mission to achieve ODF+ and ODF++ in cities and villages.
Ensure a drastic reduction in cases of tuberculosis.
Increase the doctor-population ratio to 1:1400.
Under the POSHAN Abhiyaan, aim to reduce the malnutrition level as well as accelerate the rate of reduction of malnourishment.
Further improve India’s rank in ‘Ease of Doing Business’ ranking.
Work towards improving GDP share from manufacturing sector.
Work towards doubling the total exports.
Work towards creating a single-window compliance and dispute resolution mechanism for MSMEs.
Strive to ensure reduced tax rates, higher tax collection and greater compliance.
Work towards ensuring a stable taxation regime.
Work towards increasing female workforce participation rate.
Ensure justice for Muslim women by enacting the law against triple talaq.
Work towards substantially reducing the current levels of air pollution.
Enable digital delivery of government services.
Promote and increase digital transactions.
Ensure end-to-end digitisation of government processes.
Achieve complete digitisation and modernisation of courts.
Ensure the availability of banking services within 5 kms of every individual.
Put an Indian in space in an Indian spacecraft as part of ‘Gaganyaan’ mission.
Ensure full immunisation coverage of children.
Strive to make all government buildings accessible.
Ensure the completion of six Tribal Freedom Fighters Museums.
Work towards completing the development of ‘Panchteerth’ circuit.
Work towards completely eliminating crop residue burning to reduce air pollution.
Ensure comprehensive social security coverage for all unorganised labourers including insurance, pension etc.
Complete the development of all projects under Swadesh Darshan, PRASAD and HRIDAY schemes.
Achieve the goal of Clean Ganga by 2022.
Work towards digitisation of collections in all national museums.
